chunk_summary: Adolescents with CKD or CLD face adherence challenges due to social factors, high pill burden, and body image concerns. Strategies include simplified dietary rules, discreet medication plans, and early psychological intervention. Post-transplant counseling focuses on growth, managing steroid side effects, and food safety.
learning_objectives: Understand the social and psychological barriers to dietary adherence in adolescents with CKD or CLD.
Develop strategies to simplify dietary plans and improve adherence in adolescents.
Recognize and address disordered eating behaviors and their impact on CKD/CLD management.
Counsel post-transplant patients on managing steroid side effects and ensuring food safety.
key_facts: Adolescents with CKD/CLD often struggle with adherence due to social incompatibility, high pill burden, and body image concerns.
Simplified dietary rules, such as a short 'swap' list and two non-negotiable safety rules, improve adherence.
Key safety rules include avoiding potassium chloride salt substitutes and cola-type beverages.
Steroid use post-transplant can lead to hyperglycemia and rapid weight gain, requiring reduced simple sugar intake.
Food safety is critical post-transplant, especially during the first 3–6 months when immunosuppression is highest.
reference_quote: High pill burden (binders, vitamins, alkali), school-time eating, and body image concerns can resemble disordered eating and lead to concealment ('I eat nothing') while electrolyte disturbances recur.
